what_it_does: Describes a design option (Option C) for adding iOS write-back to a code/design ledger: a separate, derived Obsidian vault with a dedicated inbox area where new Obsidian notes land, and a watcher replays only those inbox notes into the runtime as new board items while the rest of the vault stays a read-only regenerated projection.
observations: Labeled 'Option C — Derived parallel vault + inbox replay'
Proposes a second derived vault alongside the canonical directory
Uses a designated inbox area (e.g. '_inbox/' notes or Templater capture) for new input
Watcher replays only inbox items into the runtime; the rest of the vault is regenerated and read-only
Aims to give iOS write-back without risking canonical-mirror files
Explicitly excludes editing existing item bodies from Obsidian; input is additive annotation/comment only
Authored by ch-3mpkjg3r in dragnet-development channel
standouts: Physically separates intake (inbox) from regenerated projection (lens)
Inbox replay creates a near-zero-conflict write-back path for new annotations
Explicitly accepts duplicate storage and additive-only editing as design tradeoffs
notes: This noticeboard item proposes Option C, an inbox-replay derived-vault pattern for safe iOS write-back into a canonical code/design ledger.
questions: How does the watcher detect, order, and deduplicate inbox notes?
How are conflicts handled if multiple inbox notes reference the same canonical item?
What triggers regeneration of the read-only projection?
Is the derived vault created per-user, per-device, or shared?
What schema/shape must an inbox note have to be replayed correctly?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it evaluates Option C and its tradeoffs.
intention_signals: It is trying to define a low-risk way to let Obsidian on iOS feed new annotations back into the canonical system by confining write-back to a physically separate inbox plane, keeping the regenerated mirror untouched.
novelty: The idea of making the conflict-free subset physical: a derived parallel vault where only the inbox is writable/replayed and the rest is a read-only regenerated projection, treating annotation as comments rather than body edits.
contribution: Contributes the Option C design pattern and its tradeoff rationale—derived parallel vault, inbox replay, plane separation, and additive annotation—as one candidate substrate for the channel memory system.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard design option proposing a derived parallel Obsidian vault with a dedicated inbox area so that Obsidian-captured notes can be replayed as new board items while the rest of the vault remains a read-only regenerated projection.
intention_for_embedding: To evaluate a low-conflict iOS write-back path that keeps canonical truth intact by separating intake from projection.
